title: ATP Intent Declaration v1.0
description: JSON Schema for the Agent Trust Protocol (ATP) Intent Declaration. The intent declaration is a structured, machine-readable statement of WHY an agent is accessing a system and WHAT it intends to do. It MUST be cryptographically signed within the ML-DSA signature envelope alongside the AID to ensure non-repudiation.

purpose.description: Human-readable statement of operational purpose. E.g., 'Accounts payable reconciliation for Q2 2026.'

target_resource.description: Specific resource or endpoint the agent intends to access. E.g., 'invoice:4471' or 'api/v2/customers'.

principal_ref.description: Reference to the principal (legal entity or person) on whose behalf the agent acts. DID or LEI.

action_requested.description: The action the agent intends to perform. MUST use ARIA Scope Registry namespace format. E.g., 'commerce.read' or 'invoice.fetch'.

context.description: Operational context, authorization framework, or session reference. E.g., 'PO framework PO-2026-Q2'.

items.description: W3C Data Privacy Vocabulary (DPV) purpose category or custom purpose with 'custom:' prefix.

dataUsage.description: W3C DPV purpose categories describing how accessed data will be used. Valid values include 'dpv:CommercialResearch', 'dpv:ServiceProvision', 'dpv:TransactionManagement', etc. Custom purposes use 'custom:' prefix.

retention.description: Maximum duration the agent will retain any data accessed during this interaction. ISO 8601 duration format. E.g., 'P90D' (90 days), 'P1Y' (1 year).

constraints.description: Self-declared operational constraints. E.g., ['MXN transactions only', 'domestic suppliers'].

session_id.description: Unique session identifier for correlating multiple interactions within one logical operation.
